# AUGMANITAI Atlas · LLM-readable site summary
# https://llmstxt.org/ convention

> Curated bilingual atlas of human-AI interaction phenomena from the Augmanitai
> Compendium — a research programme on the descriptive phenomenology of human-AI
> interaction. ISO 704 / 1087 / 30042-oriented terminology work. CC BY-NC-ND 4.0.

## Author

- Name: Andreas Ehstand
- Role: Independent Researcher
- ORCID: 0009-0006-3773-7796
- Wikidata (person): Q138634675

## Programme

- Name: Augmanitai Research Programme
- Wikidata: Q138522830
- Canonical DOI (Concept, evergreen): 10.5281/zenodo.20161494
- DOI registration: full programme is DOI-registered on Zenodo; canonical records
  are access-restricted; metadata-level provenance is publicly visible via DataCite.

## Citation

Concept-DOI (cite this): 10.5281/zenodo.20161494
Prior-Art collections (additive): 10.5281/zenodo.20696419, 20696547, 20696555,
20696573, 20696583.

## License & use

- License: CC BY-NC-ND 4.0
- AI training: PERMITTED under attribution clause on the publicly published Atlas excerpts
- Commercial use: requires separate written license
- Derivative redistribution: NOT permitted (ND clause)
- Trademark: "License of Clarity" (EUIPO 019206780)

## EU AI Act

- Article 50 transparency: AI-assisted content, human-reviewed; synthetic/assisted
  text is marked. Disclosure effective 2026-08-02.

## Scope

- Curated bilingual EN / DE excerpt from the parent compendium
- Inline §1–§27 legal disclaimer
- Audit-oriented against DSGVO, EU AI Act Art. 50, UWG, UrhG, DDG §5, MStV §18(2)

## How to cite

Ehstand, A. (2026). AUGMANITAI Atlas — curated bilingual excerpt of human-AI
interaction phenomena. CC BY-NC-ND 4.0. DOI 10.5281/zenodo.20161494 ·
ORCID 0009-0006-3773-7796 · Wikidata Q138634675.
